q: The internal resistance of fluid is called ___.
Viscosity can be thought of as a measure of a fluid's ___ (stickiness).
A fluid with large viscosity resists motion because its strong ___ forces give it a lot of internal friction.
The viscosity of liquids decreases rapidly with an increase in ___.
Viscosity does not change as the amount of matter changes, therefore it is an ___ property.
The SI unit of viscosity is pascal second ($Pa \cdot s$) which is equivalent to ___.
The other way to measure the resistive flow of a fluid under the weight of gravity is known as ___ viscosity.
___ Law states that the drag force $F$ acting upward in resistance to the fall of a spherical object is $F = 6 \pi \eta r v$.
Shear stress is defined as the ratio of ___ stress to the velocity gradient.
